page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4d family. It features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, aligning it with compute-optimized resource profiles.

The instance does not include bundled local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the configuration features zero GPUs and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, which are key architectural tradeoffs to consider for workloads requiring direct internet routing or hardware acceleration.

Due to its dedicated CPU allocation and lower memory-to-core ratio, this server type offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is designed for tasks such as batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and CI/CD pipelines where CPU execution speed is prioritized over large in-memory datasets.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4d family, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation provides a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, positioning the instance for compute-heavy workloads. The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence and relying entirely on the host CPU for processing. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of excess memory. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, distributed compute tasks, and continuous integration pipelines.
